answer: The girls are wearing the shoes.
reasoning: Subject is Adzʁabchᵂa ‘girls’ (pl); object ajmaakᵂa ‘shoes’ (lower-body); 3pl verb rәʃop’ (r- for 3pl, -op’ for lower-body garments).

answer: The nanny-goat is wearing the trousers.
reasoning: Aba ‘nanny-goat’ (3sg class with i-); ajkʷa ‘trousers’ (lower-body) → iʃop’.
Ak’asәkᵂa ‘shawls’ (upper-body); verb ʃʷʃʷup’ shows 2pl subject (ʃʷ-) and upper-body ending -up’.
Ak’ʷәmʒʷә ‘cherkeska’; 2sg subject prefix b-; upper-body garment → -up’.
Anchᵂa ‘god’ → plural Anchᵂachᵂa; ajmsәkᵂa ‘felt boots’ (lower-body); 3pl r- with -op’.

answer: Word order is Subject–Object–Verb. Verb agrees with the subject by a prefix: s- ‘I’, b- ‘you (sg)’, h- ‘we’, ʃʷ(ә)- ‘you (pl)’, r- ‘they’; various 3sg classes include a-, l-, i- depending on the noun. The verb also classifies the clothing item: upper-body garments take -up’, lower-body/footwear take -op’. Noun plurals use -kʷa or -chʷa (e.g., Ab → Abkʷa ‘billy-goats’; Aba → Abachʷa ‘sons’).
